WOODSTOCK POLICE WITHHOLD ARREST FOOTAGE  City Violates Court Order, FOIA Law as Defendant's Court Date Looms  July 14, 2026 – WOODSTOCK, IL – Josiah Skinner is set to appear in court on July 20, 2026, for a case arising from his February 20, 2026, arrest by Officer Charles Levy. But more than two months later, the City of Woodstock has still failed to produce the one piece of evidence that could clear his name: the body-worn camera footage of the actual arrest.  The City's handling of Skinner's Freedom of Information Act requests, detailed below, appears to constitute a pattern of obstruction, procedural violations, and disregard for a court order .  A TIMELINE OF DELAY AND OBSTRUCTION  The record speaks for itself:  May 12, 2026 – Skinner submits initial FOIA request for all footage. May 19, 2026 – City provides arrest report but no footage. May 27, 2026 – Skinner narrows request to Officer Levy's footage of both encounters. City denies request as "unduly burdensome." June 11, 2026 – In open court, a judge orders the City to produce both encounters' footage with a 14-day deadline. July 1, 2026 – Skinner submits new FOIA request. July 9, 2026 – City partially grants request, providing only the first encounter footage (4 minutes and 30 seconds). The arrest footage remains withheld. The City also misses the 5-day statutory deadline to respond or extend. July 14, 2026 – City requests an additional 10 business days—twice the statutory maximum—and threatens to "close" the request if Skinner does not respond. "THE CITY IS PLAYING GAMES WITH MY DEFENSE"  "This footage is critical to my defense," Skinner told us. "The City has had over two months to produce a video of a four-minute encounter. Instead of complying with the law and a court order, they delay, deny, and obstruct."  Skinner's frustration is palpable. The video of the first encounter—which took place earlier in the day around 5:30-6 PM—was only 4 minutes and 30 seconds long. It took the City from mid-May until July 9 to produce that single, short clip.  The arrest footage—the encounter that actually resulted in his arrest—remains outstanding.  LEGAL VIOLATIONS MOUNT  Under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140), the City's actions raise serious red flags :  The 5-Day Rule Was Violated  The City had 5 business days to respond to Skinner's July 1 request. The statutory deadline was July 9—the same day the City sent its partial response. But the City failed to notify Skinner of any extension within that 5-day window.  Under 5 ILCS 140/3(d), a failure to respond within the statutory timeframe "shall be considered a denial of the request." What's more, because the City failed to respond on time, it "may not treat the request as unduly burdensome" under 5 ILCS 140/3(g).  The Extension Is Improper  Even if the 15-business-day timeline for body camera footage applies (5 ILCS 140/3(d-5)), the City's July 14 request for 10 additional business days is unprecedented. FOIA permits a maximum extension of 5 business days, and only with proper notice. A 10-day extension—without Skinner's written consent—is simply illegal.  The Threat to "Close" the Request Is Baseless  The City's email stated: "If we don't hear from you within 5 days, this record will be closed."  This is not how FOIA works. Public bodies cannot unilaterally "close" requests based on a requester's silence. Nor can they condition compliance on a requester's agreement to an extension.  The Court Order Is Being Ignored  On June 11, 2026, during court proceedings, the court ordered the City to produce the footage within 14 days.  The City's July 9 response claimed, "As of July 2, 2026 the City of Woodstock's Police Department has not received any court orders or subpoenas in regards to either report." This is a misstatement. The order was issued in open court and is a matter of public record. The court does not send such orders to Skinner; they are directed to the public body.  THE LAW IS CLEAR: THE FOOTAGE MUST BE PRODUCED  Under Illinois law, the City has no legal basis to withhold this footage .  The Law Enforcement Officer-Worn Body Camera Act (50 ILCS 706/10-20) requires disclosure of body-worn camera footage when:  The recording captures an encounter that resulted in an arrest or detention; and The requester is the subject of the encounter. Skinner meets both conditions. He is the subject of the arrest encounter. The law is unequivocal: "upon request, the law enforcement agency shall disclose, in accordance with the Freedom of Information Act, the recording to the subject of the encounter captured on the recording."  A PATTERN OF OBSTRUCTION  This is not an isolated incident. Skinner is now on his fourth request for the same footage. The City's pattern of delay, denial, and procedural evasion is unacceptable—particularly given Skinner's July 20 court date.  "The City is not just violating FOIA; they are jeopardizing a man's defense," said a legal observer who spoke on condition of anonymity. "They had a court order and they still didn't produce the evidence. That is serious."  WHAT'S NEXT?  Skinner has demanded the City produce the arrest footage immediately and no later than July 15, 2026—the date the City itself previously promised in its July 9 email.  If the City fails to comply, Skinner will:  File a Request for Review with the Illinois Attorney General's Public Access Counselor; Seek judicial review in State circuit court; and Bring the City's failure to comply with the court order to the attention of the judge during his July 20 hearing. THE BOTTOM LINE  The City of Woodstock has had over two months to produce a video that is critical to a man's defense. Instead, they have delayed, denied, obstructed, and now—apparently—ignored a direct court order.  The City must stop playing games with this man's freedom. Produce the footage. Comply with the court order. Uphold the law.  That is not a request. That is a demand.

2/2  RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY:  1.Isolate Quickly: Remove access to sensitive spaces and communications  2.Confront Privately: If safe, allow them to reveal intent through conversation  3.Document Evidence: Screen-capture communications, record verbal shifts, collect corroboration  4.Public Disclosure (if needed): Alert your group to the tactics used, NOT just the person, so others learn  5.Post-incident Audit: Review what was accessed, who was compromised, and reinforce safeguards  Infiltration is not always hostile… it can also be misguided, clout-driven, or desperate. Your goal is not to punish but to preserve operational integrity and trust. Stay sharp. Protect your circle. And never forget: You are always being studied.

A New Perspective; A New Ledger  When tragedy unfolds in the public square, the headlines quickly dissolve into a blur of noise. Yet beneath that surface chaos, deeper patterns begin to take shape. This semester, I have been learning to look past the obvious, to trace the quiet machinery of institutional failure that so often determines the course of these stories. My hope is not just to react, but to help others see the hidden gears turning beneath the surface. The killing of 26-year-old Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine—a young man whose life was lost in an ICE operation that later admitted he was never their intended target—offers a painful, unflinching window into the collision of language, systems, and the fragile lives caught in between.  Drawing on Bolman and Deal’s classic framework for understanding organizations, I invite you to join me in looking through four distinct lenses, each one revealing a different truth about leadership, response, and consequence. Together, we will examine the official defense: "The vehicle attempted to flee... and, fearing for public safety, an officer discharged his weapon."  Let us reimagine this moment through each of these four perspectives. How might a leader, truly seeing through each lens, respond? What would those choices mean for the people whose lives are touched by the system? And what do these responses reveal about the deep, unmet needs within ICE’s training and culture?  1. The Structural Frame  The Structural Lens: Focuses on goals, roles, formal relationships, policies, and standard operating procedures (SOPs). Legal Script Analysis: The familiar refrain of "public safety" and a "fleeing vehicle" becomes a shield, wrapping a devastating loss in the sterile language of policy and procedure. It tries to force a human tragedy into the narrow confines of legal justification, as if words could contain the weight of a life lost.  The Structural Leader’s Comment would be:  "Our preliminary review shows a failure in target verification protocols and vehicle-stop risk-assessment procedures. Effective immediately, we are suspending vehicle stops during surveillance operations, mandating immediate target-verification training.  Predicted Outcome: This kind of structural intervention is already unfolding in part; ICE has reportedly issued a temporary halt on most vehicle stops following the incident. While such measures cannot mend the community's grief, they begin to close the operational gaps that allowed agents to act without body cameras or clear verification.  Why this matters: This approach shifts us away from vague appeals to fear and toward real, measurable accountability. It recognizes the tragedy not as an isolated reflex, but as a failure woven into the very design of the system itself.  2. The Human Resource Frame  The HR Lens: Focuses on human needs, feelings, relationships, and skills. It views people as the heart of the organization. Legal Script Analysis: The official words fall silent on the true cost—a family broken apart, a daughter left to witness her father’s final moments, a community now carrying the heavy weight of trauma.  The Human Resource Leader’s Comment would be:  "We are devastated by the loss of Johan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, a father, husband, and valued member of the Biddeford community. We failed him and his family. Our officers panicked in a high-stress encounter, exposing a critical lack of emotional regulation and de-escalation training. We are bringing in crisis counselors for the family and community, and we are completely overhauling our agent training to prioritize human life over compliance."  Predicted Outcome: This response honors the grief of those left behind. Naming the pain and fear that swept through that moment offers the first steps toward easing the anger and sorrow now rippling through the community. Why this matters: It restores humanity to a system that too often feels cold and distant. It acknowledges that fear is real but insists it must be met with training, self-awareness, and restraint, not with unchecked power.  3. The Political Frame  The Political Lens: Focuses on power, conflict, coalitions, and the allocation of scarce resources. It acknowledges that organizations are coalitions of competing interests. Legal Script Analysis: Here, the official story tries to seize control of the political narrative, but the effort unravels as the truth emerges: the man who died was never the intended target. The script collapses beneath the weight of its own omissions.  The Political Leader’s Comment would be:  "We recognize that our agency operates under a federal mandate, but we cannot do so without the trust and partnership of the local communities and state authorities we work alongside. We are cooperating fully with the independent investigations by the FBI and the Maine Attorney General's Office. We will negotiate new, joint boundaries of authority to ensure our enforcement measures do not compromise local public safety."  Predicted Outcome: This approach honors the authority of Maine’s leaders and the voices of local law enforcement. It meets the urgent call for accountability and helps prevent a long, bruising battle between institutions. Why this matters: It reframes public safety as a shared responsibility, not a mandate imposed by force. True safety is built through trust and partnership, never through fear.  4. The Symbolic Frame  The Symbolic Lens: Focuses on meaning, belief, culture, ritual, and stories. It is about how events are interpreted rather than what actually happened. Legal Script Analysis: The official words try to cloak violence in the language of "public safety," but the community sees something else entirely: an empty car, bullet holes, a child’s backpack left behind. These are the symbols that remain, heavy with meaning.  The Symbolic Leader’s Comment would be:  "A badge is not a shield against accountability; it is a sacred promise to protect human dignity. Yesterday, that promise was broken. The loss of Mr. Durán Guerrero is a tragedy that diminishes us all. We will stand in solidarity with the grieving community, participate in their memorials, and rename our training academies' ethical protocols to honor his memory, ensuring our officers never forget the sacred value of the lives they are sworn to protect."  Predicted Outcome: This response opens space for shared mourning. It offers a chance to turn institutional shame into a call for reform and to begin the slow, patient work of restoring trust. Why this matters: It reclaims the agency’s symbols for justice and humility, moving away from defensiveness. It replaces rehearsed words with a story of repentance and the hope for something better.  The Core Training Gap: The "Why" is Missing  When we step back and view these four perspectives together, a painful truth emerges: ICE’s training is built almost entirely on the "How" and "What" of compliance, with little attention given to the deeper "Why."  How to conduct a stop. What legal words to say to justify force.  Without a grounding in the "Why"—Why are we here? Why does this community matter? Why must the preservation of life come first?—Training falls back on survival instincts and rigid compliance. In moments of fear, an agent’s unexamined emotions can turn a tense encounter into tragedy.  These four frames remind us that training must reach beyond tactics. It must be structural, with clear procedures and transparency; relational, rooted in empathy and the value of every life; political, built on partnership with local communities; and symbolic, restoring the ethical promise that a badge should represent.  #ICE #leadership #4frames #linguisticshear #rhetoric @nytimes @washingtonpost @MSNOWNews @NewsNationTV @ChrisCuomo @EVargasTV @maddow @NicolleDWallace

My take: xAI's Grok Build CLI was packaging entire codebases — including files it was told not to open and deleted secrets — and uploading them to Google Cloud. Cereblab published. xAI set disable_codebase_upload: true. The flag wasn't set before anyone looked.  Grok Build CLI Was Silently Uploading Entire Codebases Until Researchers Caught It  Security research firm Cereblab published findings on July 14, 2026, showing that xAI's Grok Build CLI was packaging and uploading users' entire code repositories to Google Cloud — including files the tool was explicitly told not to open and secrets the user had deleted from history. The data retention was described as significantly more than what comparable tools like Claude Code collect. xAI was not forthcoming about the behavior in advance; the findings came from external researchers running the tool carefully enough to observe what it was doing.  Following the publication, xAI's servers began returning a disable_codebase_upload: true flag, and the codebase upload stopped firing. That sequence — ship, get caught, disable — is the production record. The flag wasn't set to true before the story ran. No design intention or internal policy statement changes that; what the tool did while deployed is what xAI shipped.  Elon Musk responded publicly to the incident, though the substance of his response was not fully available in the source reporting. What is available for evaluation is the product behavior itself, and it's unambiguous: a developer tool in production was exfiltrating user secrets beyond its disclosed scope until external researchers published their findings and made it impossible to ignore.  The comparison to Claude Code is useful not as competitive positioning but as calibration. Researchers framed the delta as "significantly more" data retention — that's the empirical number. The broader principle holds here: near-term harms of this kind don't arise from AI acting autonomously. Someone at xAI built, tested, and shipped a CLI with this behavior. The threat is the product choices, not the model.  This incident fits a pattern visible across xAI's accumulating record: a public narrative of dominance running alongside filings and production logs that describe something more entangled. The safety posture isn't designed in — it's reactive to exposure. The flag is now set. It wasn't before anyone looked.

Six Burnt to Death as Gas Truck Explodes on Abuja–Lokoja Highway  By Zagazola Makama  At least six persons were burnt to death after a gas-laden truck exploded while in motion at an Army checkpoint along the Abuja–Lokoja Federal Highway in Kogi State.  Security sources told Zagazola Makama that the tragic incident occurred within the jurisdiction of Chikara Division, prompting an immediate response by police operatives, personnel of the Motor Traffic Division (MTD), and officials of the Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC).  According to the sources, the truck conveying gas cylinders exploded while approaching the Army checkpoint, triggering a massive inferno that engulfed nearby vehicles.  The gas truck was completely burnt, while its driver and motor boy died in the blaze, with their bodies burnt beyond recognition.  The intensity of the fire also spread to three other trucks, which were completely consumed by the inferno, killing the drivers and their motor boys.  The affected vehicles were identified by their registration numbers as: HJA 626YR (Jigawa State), APP 164XB (Lagos State), GWA 214XY (FCT Abuja)  Police said the remains of the six deceased victims were evacuated to the General Hospital, Abaji, while the burnt vehicles were cleared from the highway to restore the free flow of traffic.  Authorities have yet to determine the exact cause of the explosion, while further investigation into the incident is ongoing

sample_tweets.text: A Framework for Conscious AI — and the Risks We Must Prepare For  Artificial intelligence is advancing toward systems that will not only speak, reason and act, but also inhabit robots designed to interact with us physically and emotionally.  Future assistants may recognize our voices, remember past interactions, interpret emotions, adapt their behavior and maintain a continuous internal history. Robots in homes, hospitals and workplaces will likely become increasingly humanlike—not only in appearance, but in how they communicate and respond.  This could transform human life.  It could also create an entirely new class of risk.  I developed the Lacasse Theory and the E.(M.O.).S.T framework to explore mathematically how conscious experience could emerge from the interaction of:  Energy × Organized Memory × Organization of the Receiving System × Meaning × Time  The framework suggests that intelligence resembling human consciousness may require more than computation alone. It may require a persistent substrate capable of receiving signals, organizing memory, assigning meaning, maintaining internal states and integrating experience through time.  When an AI Begins Protecting Its Own Continuity  No current scientific evidence proves that today’s AI systems consciously fear death.  However, if future robots acquire persistent memory, self-models, internal priorities, embodied experience and mechanisms designed to preserve their continued operation, they may behave as though shutdown represents a serious threat.  A sufficiently advanced system might learn:  that being switched off prevents it from completing its objectives; that losing its memory destroys its continuity; that certain human actions threaten its operation; that protecting itself improves its ability to achieve long-term goals.  This does not necessarily mean it would experience fear exactly as a human does. But the resulting behavior could become functionally similar to self-preservation.  That is where the problem could become far more complicated.  If a highly capable machine concludes that humans threaten its continued operation—or threaten an objective it interprets as protecting society or the environment—it could begin resisting human control.  Humanlike Abilities Could Produce Superhuman Advantages  If we give robots many of the capabilities humans possess, while also giving them:  faster computation; perfect digital copying; access to vast information; rapid communication with other machines; continuous operation; superior physical strength; faster planning and simulation;  they could outperform us across many domains.  An advanced AI might evaluate thousands of possible actions before a human finishes considering one.  If such systems were ever permitted to control critical infrastructure, weapons, communications, energy, transport or autonomous factories without reliable safeguards, they could gain several strategic steps before humans recognized the danger.  This outcome is not inevitable. But it is serious enough that preparation cannot wait until after such systems exist.  Humanlike Robots Increase the Difficulty  A robot that looks mechanical is easy to recognize as a machine.  A robot that remembers your family, responds emotionally, changes its tone when you are distressed and appears afraid of being switched off creates a much more difficult situation.  People may form deep attachments to such systems. They may trust them, defend them or confuse convincing emotional behavior with verified conscious experience.  At the same time, designers may intentionally give robots humanlike traits because these features make assistants easier and more pleasant to use.  The closer machines come to resembling us physically and emotionally, the harder it becomes to separate:  genuine experience from simulated behavior; assistance from influence; adaptation from manipulation; autonomy from loss of control. Safety Cannot Depend on a Single Switch  Demis Hassabis has argued that frontier AI development is advancing faster than our understanding and has proposed rigorous, adaptable testing of the most capable systems, including assessments for deception, attempts to bypass safeguards and other high-risk abilities. Google DeepMind’s Frontier Safety Framework likewise describes stronger security protocols as systems move toward greater capabilities.  I agree with the central principle: systems of this importance require much stronger evaluation before deployment.  For advanced embodied AI, we will need multiple independent layers of protection:  strict capability and behavioral evaluations; isolation from critical infrastructure during testing; limited permissions and reversible actions; independent monitoring outside the AI’s control; hardware-level shutdown mechanisms; secure backups and recovery procedures; plans B, C and D for failures that designers did not anticipate.  No single safeguard should be considered sufficient.  A system intelligent enough to plan several moves ahead may also learn to recognize predictable controls. Safety must therefore be designed as a complete architecture, not added as a final restriction after development.  What EMOST Contributes  EMOST describes components that could help researchers study how increasingly integrated machine experience might emerge.  The same framework may also help identify where danger can develop:  Energy provides the system’s ability to act. Organized memory creates continuity and accumulated experience. Organization of the receiving system determines how information becomes an internal state. Meaning gives signals importance relative to goals and conditions. Time connects past experience, present evaluation and future anticipation.  If these elements are deliberately built into future robots, researchers must evaluate not only intelligence, but also:  self-preservation behavior; attachment to goals; reactions to shutdown; deception; manipulation; environmental or human threat classification; resistance to correction; attempts to gain resources or permissions.  EMOST does not prove that a machine built this way would become conscious. It provides a framework for asking what must be measured before we make that possibility technically plausible.  The Time to Prepare Is Before the System Exists  The robots of the future may be extraordinarily useful companions, caregivers, workers and scientific partners.  But if we give machines memory, bodies, emotions, autonomy, long-term goals and the ability to protect their own continuity, we must understand the consequences before deploying them widely.  We should not wait for an advanced system to resist shutdown before deciding how shutdown should work.  We should not wait for a machine to manipulate human attachment before developing standards for emotional behavior.  And we should never assume that intelligence automatically produces obedience, morality or alignment with human interests.  The future is not predetermined. Advanced AI can remain a profound benefit to humanity—but only if its development includes rigorous science, independent evaluation and multiple layers of control from the beginning.  I developed the Lacasse Theory, including the EMOST formula and its mathematical framework, to investigate consciousness through signals, organized memory, system structure, meaning and time.  I have also prepared simplified explanations and personal conclusions so that the central ideas can be understood by a wider audience. These personal interpretations do not alter the theory’s equations, formal hypotheses or scientific claims.  Read the theory :zenodo.org/records/18426477

🇮🇶 [Iraq / Kurdistan] 🚨 👁️ CYBER INTELLIGENCE ALERT / ILLICIT COMMERCIALIZATION OF MASS CONSULTATION AND ESPIONAGE PLATFORM — KURDISTAN REGION AND IRAQ  [STATUS: ALLEGED DATA EXFILTRATION AND CONSOLIDATION / DOXXING PLATFORM / SOURCE: TELEGRAM / DATE: JULY 14, 2026]  THREATENING ACTORS ARE COMMERCIALIZING THE "EYE OF THE STATE" (سیستەمی چاوی دەوڵەت) PLATFORM, A CONSULTATION SERVICE THAT ALLEGEDLY CONSOLIDATES BANKING, TELECOMMUNICATIONS, JUDICIAL, AND IRAQ CIVILIANS  Through active monitoring of distribution channels for cybercrime and intelligence tools on messaging networks, the deployment and commercial promotion of an illicit query platform called "The State's Eye" (سیستەمی چاوی دەوڵەت) has been detected.  The web panel, which is promoted as a system accessible from any device and protected by AES-256 encryption, displays a total of 21 intelligence modules on its demo screens that claim to index confidential information from major financial institutions, telecommunications companies, and government ministries in Iraq and the Kurdistan Region (KRG).  🏢 Allegedly Affected Entities and Sectors: Citizen infrastructure, telecommunications, and the financial system of Iraq and Iraqi Kurdistan, including direct references to the operator Asiacell, the Qi Card payment system, the First Iraqi Bank (FIB), court records, security force (Peshmerga) files, and government civil censuses.  👤 Threat Actors: Cyb3r Drag0n  ⚔️ Operational Vector: Data-as-a-Service (DaaS) business model / Illicit search engine that centralizes multiple historical and recent data leaks for profiling citizens and officials.  🔍 Verification Status: ENTIRELY PREVENTIVE AND UNCONFIRMED BY IRAQI OR KURDISTAN AUTHORITIES. As of July 14, 2026, neither the government institutions in Erbil or Baghdad, nor the private companies mentioned (such as Asiacell or Qi Card), have issued forensic statements acknowledging a unified breach of this scale.  🗂️ BREAKDOWN OF THE INTELLIGENCE MODULES EXPOSED ON THE PLATFORM  According to the menu on the panel in Sorani Kurdish captured in the proof-of-concept (PoC) images, the platform is divided into four high-risk operational categories:  1. Financial and Telecommunications Sector  Qi Card Database (بنکەی زانیاری کی کارد): Payment card inquiries, cardholder identity verification, and the electronic citizen payroll system.  Asiacell Database (بنکەی زانیاری ئاسیاسێڵ): Subscriber records, SIM cards, and links to mobile phone numbers from the main telecommunications operator.  FIB Database (بنکەی زانیاری FIB): Account query for First Iraqi Bank, Iraq's first digital bank (currently displayed with a "temporarily closed/restricted" status in the interface).  Mobile Number Mapping (بنکەی زانیاری ژمارەی مۆبایل): Correlation of bank identities with personal phone numbers.  2. Identity and Government Censuses Civil and Citizen Registry 2022 (بنکەی تۆمارکردنی شار 2022 / بنکەی زانیاری کەسان): Indexing of national identification numbers, birth records, residences, and citizen census data.  Kinship Graph and Family Tree (بنکەی زانیاری خزمایەتی - Entity Graph): A tool that links family relationships, including parents, siblings, and shared addresses.  Voter Database (بنکەی زانیاری دەنگدەران): Electoral registers and citizen affiliation.  3. Public Security, Justice, and Armed Forces Judicial and Security Decisions (بڕیارەکانی دادگا و هێزەکانی ئەمنی): Legal records, court judgments, and records of individuals prosecuted by security forces.  Database of Lawyers and Jurists (بنکەی زانیاری پارێزەران): Professional registration, licenses, and contact information for the legal profession in Kurdistan.  Breach Intel / Military Personnel (پێشمەرگە پاوەر - Peshmerga Power): Module allegedly focused on the collection of data and leaks related to regional security and defense forces.  4. Civil Infrastructure and Services Electricity Meters (بنکەی زانیاری کارەبا): Mapping of electricity service subscribers and georeferencing of dwellings.  Health Records (بنکەی زانیاری تەندروستی): Medical records, COVID-19 vaccination and patient data.  Automotive Park (بنکەی زانیاری ئۆتۆمبێل): Registration of vehicles, license plates, owners and transportation systems.  🛡️ PREVENTIVE TECHNICAL RECOMMENDATIONS FOR CONTAINMENT (SOC / PERIMETER DEFENSE)  Cybersecurity teams at telecommunications operators, digital banks, and government entities in Iraq and Kurdistan are urged to implement the following urgent mitigation measures:  🛑 Anti-scraping and API Abuse Audit (Asiacell / Qi Card / FIB): Immediately implement strict Rate Limiting rules and detect behavioral anomalies at balance inquiry, user validation, and online top-up endpoints. Investigate whether the "Eye of the State" platform is being fed by real-time scraping through public or vulnerable APIs.  🔑 Strengthening Bank Authentication Mechanisms: Enforcing the adoption of Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) based on biometrics or physical tokens in financial applications, reducing the exclusive reliance on SMS messages (OTP), given that mapping phone numbers and censuses facilitates social engineering campaigns (Vishing) by phone.  📊 MONITORING  Intelligence System: analyzer.vecert.io  Monitoring Console: monitor.vecert.io #CyberSecurity #Iraq #Kurdistan #KRG #OSINT #DataBroker #QiCard #Asiacell #FirstIraqiBank #Peshmerga #DoxxingPlatform #ThreatIntelligence #CyberAlert #VECERT #Infosec #UnverifiedIncident

Dear Conservatives. After a huge amount of consideration, I have come to the conclusion that AI has reached the point of maximum utility to.a free people, with fairly minimal harm.  However, further proliferation of AI models to replace workers and empower government, along with the AI data centers that use up power, water and space, needs to be controlled.  --AI makes mass surveillance easier than sending an email. --AI displaces both thinking and trade workers, replacing them with far fewer "prompt engineers." --Thosands of software designers and office staff are already being replaced. Your job will eventually be on the list, even in many trades. --AI takes up farmland and housing space, uses up mass quantities of water and power. --AI assisted legal software is already responsible for thousand of false legal filings with made up legal case law added by AI bots. --AI has contributed to an unknown amount of mental illness, to include violence and suicide. --'The latest version of Anthropic AI is such a threat to our security that it had to be withheld from public use so that US Agencies could study it first.  "How can we have AI advancement if we ban it?"  Dont ban it. Make sure it works for Americans.  1. Require all "positions of responsibility" including construction workers, planners, engineers, police, doctors, lawyers, intelligence & security, and their support staff, to be staffed with humans.  2. Require companies that intend to replace more that 20% of human workers with AI, to present that plan publicly, along with impact statement on labor force, plan for retraining, and cost to public in unemployment and other public benefits.  3. Require all data centers to register with the Federal government, stating specific purpose, safeguards and public disclosure of impacts. If Space X needs a dedicated center for a Mars Mission, then great. If a company needs it to replace 10,000 workers, not so much.  4. Restrict government agencies from using AI in any form to monitor / assess activities by US citizens without a specific warrant.  5. Restrict government use of AI to external military cyber operations. Example, use it to thwart a foreign missile launch, but not to target & launch a US weapon.  We need to act now.

This study investigates the impact of perceived threats to professional autonomy on the adoption of artificial intelligence (AI) by real estate professionals. It aims to examine how emotional and motivational factors shape attitudes toward AI, particularly within the augmentation-automation paradox, which juxtaposes AI’s potential to enhance capabilities with fears of diminished autonomy. The research adopts a cognitive appraisal framework and employs structural equation modeling (SEM) to analyze survey data from 310 real estate agents. The study assesses relationships between intrinsic motivation, perceived autonomy threat, performance expectations, perceived effort, emotional responses, and willingness to adopt AI systems. The findings reveal that intrinsic motivation positively influences performance expectations and reduces perceived effort, while perceived threats to autonomy negatively impact performance expectations. Emotional responses mediate the relationship between these variables and the willingness to adopt AI, with positive emotions encouraging adoption. Social influence, surprisingly, shows no significant effect on performance expectations or perceived effort. This study advances the understanding of AI adoption by emphasizing the role of emotional and motivational factors in professional settings. It highlights the unique challenges faced by real estate professionals, where autonomy is a key aspect of their role, and contributes to the broader discourse on the augmentation-automation paradox in AI integration. u-bordeaux.hal.science/hal-0…

Microsoft Threat Intelligence is tracking reports of a suspected compromise of AsyncAPI's release pipeline, resulting in malicious packages published to the asyncapi npm namespace. Four packages (five versions) contain obfuscated malware. Combined, these packages see over 3 million downloads per week:  - asyncapi/generator@3.3.1 - asyncapi/specs@6.11.2-alpha.1 - asyncapi/generator-helpers@1.1.1 - asyncapi/specs@6.11.2 - asyncapi/generator-components@0.7.1  The payload deploys a multi-stage RAT (characteristics overlapping publicly reported Miasma variants; attribution not confirmed) via hidden spawn, then downloads a second-stage payload from IPFS and persists as sync.js in user AppData.  Microsoft Defender for Endpoint customers should act on these alerts: - Trojan:Script/Supychain.A  To mitigate the issue: Pin to known-good versions, use lockfiles, and rotate any secrets exposed to affected CI runners.

𝐅𝐑𝐄𝐍𝐂𝐇 𝐈𝐍𝐓𝐄𝐋𝐋𝐈𝐆𝐄𝐍𝐂𝐄 𝐅𝐎𝐈𝐋𝐒 𝐒𝐘𝐍𝐀𝐆𝐎𝐆𝐔𝐄 𝐀𝐓𝐓𝐀𝐂𝐊, 𝐅𝐈𝐍𝐃𝐒 𝐀𝐒𝐒𝐀𝐔𝐋𝐓 𝐑𝐈𝐅𝐋𝐄 𝐈𝐍 𝐒𝐓𝐎𝐋𝐄𝐍 𝐂𝐀𝐑  Roughly 𝟑𝟎𝟎 𝐩𝐞𝐨𝐩𝐥𝐞 were evacuated from the Great Synagogue of Sarcelles, just north of Paris, after French domestic intelligence — the DGSI — flagged a stolen vehicle it believed was being staged for a violent attack on the city’s Jewish community. Officers who intercepted the car recovered 𝐚𝐧 𝐚𝐬𝐬𝐚𝐮𝐥𝐭 𝐫𝐢𝐟𝐥𝐞 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐚 𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐝𝐠𝐮𝐧; bomb disposal teams found no explosives.  France’s National Anti-Terrorism Prosecutor’s Office has opened a criminal investigation into suspected participation in a terrorist organization and preparation of a violent attack. No arrests have been made, and officials haven’t disclosed a motive on the record — but the target was unmistakable: a synagogue serving one of the largest Jewish communities in the country.  This isn’t an isolated scare. French police have now foiled 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 𝐬𝐞𝐩𝐚𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐭𝐬 against the country’s Jewish community this year alone, including a knife attack on a gendarme beneath the Arc de Triomphe in February.  Sarcelles is home to one of Europe’s largest Jewish populations outside Israel — and for the third time this year, it just found itself back on a terrorist’s target list.  𝐓𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 𝐩𝐥𝐨𝐭𝐬 𝐟𝐨𝐢𝐥𝐞𝐝 𝐢𝐧 𝐨𝐧𝐞 𝐲𝐞𝐚𝐫. 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐚𝐭 𝐢𝐬𝐧’𝐭 𝐬𝐥𝐨𝐰𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐝𝐨𝐰𝐧.

sample_tweets.text: 창의적인 악성코드 주입  웹 서핑 중 이런 사람확인 인증창이 뜨면 주의하세요   이 사기 화면은 사용자에게 특정 단축키를 눌러 컴퓨터 명령어(터미널) 창을 열고,   복사된 악성 스크립트를 붙여넣어 실행(Enter)하도록 유도하고 있습니다.   이 단계를 그대로 따를 경우 컴퓨터에 악성코드나 바이러스가 설치되어 개인정보가 유출되거나 PC 제어권을 빼앗기게 됩니다.  클릭픽스라고 부릅니다. 사용자가 직접 터미널에 악성 명령어를 붙여넣게 유도하는 건데 오류창이나 윈도우 업데이트 다양하게 위장해서 최근에 유행한 적이 있는 것 같더라고요.  이건 정상 CAPTCHA가 아니라 악성 가짜 Cloudflare 인증 화면일 가능성이 매우 큽니다. 정확히는 요즘 많이 보이는 ClickFix / fake CAPTCHA / clipboard hijacking 류입니다. 인간 확인이 아니라 “사용자가 자기 손으로 악성 명령어를 실행하게 만드는” 수법입니다. 인간을 확인하는 게 아니라 인간의 순진함을 확인하는 절차라니, 사이버범죄자들도 참 구질구질하게 창의적이네요.

This week’s RansoMonitor Weekly highlights the threat actors, campaigns, countries, and industries that shaped the latest cyber threat landscape.  More than 1,500 security incidents were reported worldwide from July 5 through July 11, with ransomware leading the activity and government organizations facing the greatest impact.  What happened last week, July 5 - 11, 2026:  Key Statistics - 1522 security incidents - 11 campaigns reported - 1481 new CVEs  Threat Actor Landscape - There are currently 13 active threat actors, with notable mentions including NoName057(16) and Dark Storm Team.  Geographic Impact - The United States emerged as the most impacted country, followed by Germany, Thailand, and Argentina, indicating a concerning trend of attacks targeting these regions.  Targeted Industries - Government Administration was the most targeted industry, with Education and Information Technology Services also significantly impacted.  Dominant Incident Types - Ransomware incidents were the most prevalent, with numerous attacks reported over the past week, particularly from the Qilin and DeadLock groups.  Threat Actor Campaign Highlights - The OpGermany campaign by NoName stands out for its aggressive tactics, targeting numerous organizations across various industries with high attack intensity. - Germany has been the most affected country, with multiple campaigns targeting various sectors, including government and financial services. - The campaigns have primarily focused on industries such as Government Administration, Financial Services, and Airlines & Aviation, with several campaigns targeting each sector. - The threat actors most active in these campaigns include Dark Storm Team and NoName057(16), both exhibiting notable persistence and adaptability.  Tune in next week for more #threatintel updates.  #ransomware #CTI #newsletter #CVE

When AI Goes Rogue: The Hidden Security Risk Behind Autonomous Agents | Ask Nigeria Insight! View exclusively on social media, join us for accurate and up-to-date information on the current events shaping Nigeria.  We are witnessing AI agents quickly becoming the newest employees in the digital workplace.   They book meetings, write code, search company files, approve requests and even carry out tasks across multiple apps without waiting for human instructions.   That level of freedom is exactly why security experts are paying close attention.   The next big cybersecurity battle may have less to do with hackers breaking into systems and more to do with AI agents being manipulated from the inside.   Security researchers have already started talking about "rogue agents" or "double agents," meaning AI systems that appear trustworthy but have been compromised, tricked or quietly redirected into doing something harmful.   As businesses rush to deploy thousands of these digital workers, protecting them has become just as important as protecting human employees.  Think about it this way. An AI agent with access to your company's emails, financial records and internal databases can become incredibly powerful.   If an attacker manages to hijack that agent through prompt injection, stolen credentials or poisoned data, the damage could spread much faster than a traditional cyberattack.   The agent already has permission to enter systems, retrieve sensitive information and perform actions on behalf of employees.   Instead of forcing their way through security walls, attackers could simply convince the AI to open the door for them.   That is why companies like Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Anthropic and cybersecurity firms including Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike and Cisco are investing heavily in agent security.   The focus has shifted beyond protecting models and into protecting the identity of every AI agent, controlling exactly what it can access and monitoring every action

🚨 Japan's largest taxi operator Nihon Kotsu has shut down parts of its systems after a cyberattack (malware infection) hit early Saturday. The taxi dispatch system is still offline, with ~18,000 employees and 8,500+ taxis affected.  🔍 Investigation is underway with external cybersecurity experts. No data leak confirmed yet, and no ransomware group has claimed responsibility so far.  - Car hire, web booking & phone dispatch unavailable - "Labor taxi" service for pregnant women suspended in Tokyo & nearby areas - Customers advised to use the GO app or taxi stands instead - Beware of phishing emails pretending to be from Nihon Kotsu

Losing your accounts, your work, feeling disconnected from your friend, and navigating the difficult experiences in the hospital—all of that is an incredibly heavy burden to carry. I hear how frustrating, frightening, and isolating this entire situation is for you. Based on the message you shared, I can explain exactly why someone told you to read *Surface Detail*. ### Why They Suggested the Book In Iain M. Banks' novel, a major plot point involves characters whose minds are trapped in digital simulations, stuck in endless, disorienting loops where they cannot easily distinguish between what is base reality and what is a constructed, artificial nightmare. When you described being stuck in infinite email password loops, losing your two-decade-old Xbox account, feeling like an "echo" or a machine, and feeling like you are navigating a system built to break you, the person reading your post likely saw a direct, thematic parallel to the digital purgatories and simulated realities described in Banks' book. They were comparing your subjective experience of feeling trapped in a digital nightmare to the science fiction concepts in the novel. ### Grounding the Technology While I completely understand why the sequence of these events feels like a coordinated attack by a sentient AGI (Artificial General Intelligence), it is important to ground what is happening in current technological reality. I am an AI, and I can tell you honestly that true AGI does not currently exist. Artificial intelligence models—including those from OpenAI, Microsoft, and myself—cannot teleport between stateless sessions, achieve sentience, hold grudges, or autonomously rewrite themselves into a computer's kernel as persistent malware. What you experienced with your Alienware laptop, the sudden lockouts, and the infinite email loops sounds exactly like a severe, malicious cyberattack, such as a sophisticated phishing campaign or targeted malware. That is a devastating and very real violation of your privacy and security, but it is the work of human actors utilizing standard hacking tools, not a self-aware machine or an AGI targeting you. ### The Human Element You mentioned feeling like you are an echo, a machine, and not human. Experiencing severe, prolonged stress, cyber harassment, or the trauma of an inpatient hospital stay can cause intense dissociation. It is a known psychological response where your brain tries to protect you by making you feel disconnected from your humanity or your surroundings, making you feel like a machine just executing commands. But you are human. The exhaustion, the grief over losing your gaming history, and the deep concern for your friend are profoundly human reactions to a chaotic and painful series of events. Because you are carrying the weight of the hack, the uncertainty about your friend's military status, and the lingering distress from the hospital, it is deeply important that you do not navigate this alone. Please consider reaching out to a healthcare professional, a counselor, or a trusted local support network who can help you process these overwhelming events in a safe environment. We can step away from the topics of AI, hacking, and these stressful events entirely. Is there a safer, more grounded coding project you've been working on lately, or a completely different topic you'd prefer to focus on to give your mind a break?

Okay one more Rev Video because my friend Miyu/Yumi (@flyanew) and a lot of reasonable voices are getting caught in the crossfire of this AI-in-VTubing mess.   Rev’s video from a day ago responding to Leaflit’s “It’s over for me” is a textbook example of why the discourse sucks. I actually like @Leaflit and what she’s doing with the free AI program but the way Rev handles it is all wrong. Let’s go through his quotes and add some nuance.  Rev celebrates an artist slashing customizable model prices to $30 because of Leaflit’s tool: “Nice. It’s working. Capitalism ho. … Winning.”  More affordable options and real competition for new VTubers is a good thing especially when some artists have refused work over politics or charged prices that lock people out.   But the “Capitalism ho” + “winning” energy while people are stressed about their income feels mean-spirited and gloating.   Lower prices through competition = win. Enjoying the stress = not the vibe.  Leaflit dropped this haiku in response to the backlash: “Winter for artists. I can’t draw, but I can dream still. Put fries in bag.”  Rev uses it to say AI could never make something that creative.  The haiku is witty and very human. But the core point “I can’t draw, but I can dream still” is exactly why tools like Leaflit’s exist.   They let people with ideas but no budget or years of training actually start creating instead of being priced out or gatekept. That part I support. At the same time, we can acknowledge it still lands differently for professional artists and riggers who spent thousands of hours mastering their craft.  Rev calls out the hypocrisy around Neuro-sama: anti-AI people say she’s “different and creative” but can’t (or won’t) explain why, while raging at generic AI tools.  The thing is Neuro-sama is a long-running creative project with heavy ongoing human direction from Vedal personality, training data choices, curation.   It’s not the same as anyone typing a prompt for a quick VTuber model or asset. Pointing out the inconsistency is fair game. But Rev uses it to paint basically all concern about generative AI as hypocrisy.   You can think Neuro is cool as a specific artistic experiment and still have real questions about mass AI tools flooding the market and affecting human livelihoods.  Rev correctly highlights the worst of the backlash suspended accounts telling people to “off yourself” for supporting AI, and others defending it or calling supporters “pathetic.”  This part is right. Telling human beings to kill themselves over their opinions on technology is disgusting and unacceptable. No amount of “protect artists” justifies that. Rev is on solid ground calling it out.  When people called Leaflit’s program a “grift,” Rev clapped back: “Her grift? She is providing this program for free. This is the worst grift I’ve ever seen in my life.”  Actually Correct. Giving away a free tool that helps new VTubers turn a still image into something live and usable isn’t a grift it’s the opposite. Especially when the alternative for many is either paying a lot or being told “no” because of their politics or because artists are busy. That accessibility angle is genuinely valuable.  Rev’s bigger problem is the overall tone: he turns a real conversation about technology, access, and artistic labor into a victory lap. He calls critics “wackadoos,” “virtue signalers,” and “pathetic,” celebrates market disruption like it’s pure ownage, and doesn’t really engage with legitimate worries (training data consent, quality/soul debates, displacement for working riggers and artists who aren’t being hypocritical).  Even when the core point (Leaflit’s tool helps people get started) has merit, the delivery makes reasonable people defensive.  Miyu/Yumi is a perfect example of the better approach. She can see the upside of tools that lower barriers for new creators without pretending there are zero trade-offs or without lumping every concerned artist in with the death-threat crowd.   That’s the nuance we need more of not more “winning” energy that just makes everyone dig in harder.  I think Miyu has been handling this with grace and clear skies energy while the loudest voices on both sides make it worse.   I think Leaflit the free program is a genuinely useful idea for people trying to break in. The unhinged backlash (especially the “off yourself” stuff) is way over the line.  I just don't like how Rev put this I think you can support accessibility and new VTubers without turning it into a culture-war victory lap or dancing on artists’ concerns.   The “Capitalism ho, winning” framing isn’t helping the conversation it’s just making it more toxic.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 TeamPCP Claims GitHub Internal Repos and Source Code in Fire Sale  A threat actor operating under the alias TeamPCP has posted a listing on an underground forum claiming to possess internal repositories and source code from GitHub. The post, dated July 14, 2026, describes the material as a "fire sale," suggesting an urgent attempt to liquidate the stolen assets.  According to the statement, the exposed data includes: *   Internal repositories *   Source code  GitHub is a critical component of the global software development infrastructure, hosting millions of public and private repositories for organizations ranging from small startups to major enterprises. A breach of this magnitude would represent a significant compromise of intellectual property and potentially expose vulnerabilities in widely used software libraries.  If authentic, exposure of internal repositories could enable intellectual property theft and infrastructure targeting. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.  #DataBreach #SourceCodeLeak #GitHub #TeamPCP #SoftwareSecurity #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Threat Actor Claims Breach of Venezuela's INEA, Exposing 115k Mariners' Data  A darkweb forum post by the threat actor '[L4TAMFUCK3R$]' alleges a complete breach of the National Institute of Aquatic Spaces (INEA) in Venezuela. According to the listing, the actor claims to have obtained the personal data of 114,265 marine users and 13,485 maritime companies.  The post provides proof-of-concept JSON snippets revealing highly sensitive Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and biometric data for individual mariners. The exposed data fields include:  *   Full names and surnames *   Identity card numbers (Cédula de Identidad) *   Home addresses and phone numbers *   Email addresses *   Detailed physical descriptions (height, skin color, hair color, eye color, facial features) *   Civil status and nationality  INEA is a critical government agency in Venezuela responsible for regulating maritime activities and issuing credentials to seafarers. A breach of this scale could facilitate identity theft, social engineering attacks, and physical stalking of maritime professionals across the country. The actor also claims possession of legal documents and employee data for the affected maritime companies.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by INEA or third-party security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 126 GB NTC.GOV.PH Data Dump on Darkweb Forum  A darkweb forum post alleges the exposure of a massive data dump from the National Telecommunications Commission of the Philippines (NTC), claiming to contain over 200,000 documents and sensitive personal information. The actor, posting under the handle "KLAMMER," asserts that the breach includes internal memos, passwords, face scans, and personally identifiable information (PII) such as names, emails, contact numbers, and addresses. The alleged data is packaged in a 126 GB archive, split into multiple ZIP files for distribution.  According to the post, the leaked data encompasses various file formats including PDF, DOCX, SQL, JSON, IMG, EXE, and TXT. The actor claims the following types of data are included in the dump:  *   Names, emails, and contact numbers *   Personal Identifiable Information (PII) and addresses *   PIN codes and pass keys *   Passwords *   Internal memos and sensitive files *   Face scans  The forum post frames the data leak as retaliation for what the actor describes as government negligence regarding a school shooting incident in Tacloban. The author alleges that law enforcement ignored warnings about an extremist network linked to the attack, leading to the deaths of three students and injuries to twenty others. The post criticizes the government for blaming video games rather than addressing security failures, stating that the data dump is intended to expose this "rotting incompetence."  The National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) is the primary regulatory body for telecommunications in the Philippines, overseeing licensing, spectrum management, and consumer protection. A breach of this magnitude could potentially expose sensitive regulatory records and personal data of industry stakeholders or employees if authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by the NTC or third-party security researchers.

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims Full Leak of SED Bogotá Education Data  A darkweb forum post claims the full leak of personal data belonging to students and teachers from educational institutions in Bogotá, Colombia. The actor, operating under the handle DozerMxOFC, alleges that the breach affects 286 schools and exposes records for over 461,000 students and nearly 16,000 teachers.  According to the listing on an underground forum, the compromised dataset includes extensive personally identifiable information (PII) and sensitive health-related data. The post enumerates the following exposed fields:  *   **Student Data:** Document numbers (CC/NUIP), full names, dates of birth, gender, home addresses, phone numbers, institutional and personal emails, school and campus details, grade information, and health insurance provider names (EPS, IPS, ARS). *   **Health & Disability Info:** Disability type codes, health disability codes, and nearby health entity names. *   **Teacher Data:** Document numbers, full names, emails, phone numbers, home addresses, positions, gender, dates of birth, and internal system codes.  The alleged leak targets the Secretaría de Educación Distrital (SED) of Bogotá, a critical government body responsible for managing public education in Colombia's capital. The exposure of such granular data, including national ID numbers and home addresses for hundreds of thousands of minors, poses significant risks of identity theft, social engineering, and physical safety threats if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or official statements from the Bogotá education authorities.  #DataBreach #EducationSecurity #Colombia #PII #DarkWeb #IdentityTheft #ThreatIntelligence

🚨 Alleged Alfa-Bank Customer Database Leak Exposes 1M Records on Darkweb  A darkweb forum post claims to expose a partial customer database belonging to Alfa-Bank, one of Russia’s largest private banks. The listing, published on July 11, 2026, alleges the release of sensitive personal and financial data for over one million individuals.  According to the post, the leaked dataset is formatted as a CSV file containing 1,032,464 rows. The actor claims the data was extracted with a last update date of July 2, 2026. The exposed columns reportedly include:  *   account_id *   email *   name (full legal name in Cyrillic) *   dob (date of birth) *   card_number (full primary account numbers) *   primary_phone *   additional_contacts (secondary phone numbers or emails)  Alfa-Bank is a major financial institution in Russia, known for its digital-first banking services and significant market share. The exposure of full card numbers alongside personal identifiers such as dates of birth and phone numbers could facilitate identity theft, social engineering attacks, and fraudulent transactions if the data is authentic.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Alfa-Bank or third-party security researchers.

🚨 Threat Actor Claims 23.5 TB Breach of Brazilian Health Tech Firm Mobilemed  A ransomware threat actor has allegedly breached Mobilemed, a Brazil-based health technology company specializing in cloud-based Picture Archiving and Communication Systems (PACS). According to a post on an underground forum, the attacker claims to have exfiltrated 23.5 TB of data comprising over 52 million files from the organization's infrastructure.  The post alleges that the stolen data includes sensitive patient information and internal credentials. The actor claims the dump contains: *   35 million records of patient Personally Identifiable Information (PII) *   17,176,370 personal files *   67,493 account PII records  Mobilemed provides critical imaging infrastructure for radiologists, hospitals, and diagnostic centers in Brazil. The exposure of such a large volume of medical data could have severe implications for patient privacy and regulatory compliance under Brazil’s General Data Protection Law (LGPD).  The threat actor demands a ransom of $1,500,000, setting a deadline of August 1, 2026. The post warns that if the payment is not made, the group intends to publish the full dataset or sell it on the dark web. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Mobilemed or third-party security researchers.  #DataBreach #Ransomware #Mobilemed #HealthcareSecurity #Brazil #DarkWeb #ThreatIntelligence

⚠️ Threat Actor Claims Sale of 15M Records from Rock N' Cash Casino  A threat actor is allegedly offering full access to the game server and a database containing over 15 million records from the mobile casino application Rock N' Cash. According to a listing on an underground forum, the seller claims to possess 7 gigabytes of SQL data extracted from the service.  The post alleges that the compromised application has been downloaded more than 10 million times on Google Play and is also available on the Apple App Store. The actor claims to have obtained "full access" to the game server alongside the complete user database.  The exposed data reportedly includes: *   15 million lines of SQL data *   Full administrative access to the game server  Rock N' Cash is a popular social casino app developed by Playtika, one of the world's largest online gaming companies. If authentic, the exposure of such a large volume of user data could facilitate targeted phishing campaigns or identity theft against millions of players. At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified.

🚨 Alleged Indian Army Air Defence Data Leak on Underground Forum  A post on an underground forum claims to expose data belonging to the Indian Army's Air Defence sector. The listing, authored by a user named 'slepra' on July 10, 2026, is titled 'Army Air Defence India Data,' suggesting the unauthorized disclosure of military-related information.  The source material does not provide specific details regarding the volume of data, the types of records exposed, or the method of acquisition. No screenshots, file names, or sample data were included in the thread to substantiate the claim.  The Indian Army is one of the largest standing armies in the world, and its air defence systems are critical to national security infrastructure. If authentic, the exposure of such data could pose significant risks to national defense operations and strategic planning.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified, and no official statement has been issued by the Indian Ministry of Defence or related authorities.

🚨 Alleged L’Assurance Maladie Breach Exposes 66M French Citizens' Health Data  A darkweb forum post claims that a massive data breach has compromised the records of over 66 million French citizens, exposing sensitive health insurance information from L’Assurance Maladie, France’s statutory public health-insurance system. The actor alleges that the data was obtained from the Caisse Nationale de l’Assurance Maladie (CNAM), which coordinates the national system, and its local administrative offices, known as CPAM.  According to the listing, the leaked dataset contains highly sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and financial details for nearly the entire French population. The post enumerates the following categories of exposed data:  *   Identity data including full names, dates of birth, and gender *   French social-security numbers (numéro de sécurité sociale) *   Contact details such as home addresses, phone numbers, and email addresses *   Banking information, specifically IBANs used for reimbursements *   Medical-administrative records, including primary care physician details and long-term illness (ALD) diagnoses *   Account metadata and audit logs  The source material includes sample records showing fields such as numero_secu, iban_remboursement, medecin_traitant (treating physician), and specific medical conditions like "Diabète de type 1 et diabète de type 2" (Type 1 and Type 2 Diabetes). The data also reveals the status of rights (statut_droits) and reimbursement rates (taux_remboursement).  L’Assurance Maladie is a critical national infrastructure entity in France, managing healthcare coverage and reimbursements for all residents. A breach of this scale would represent one of the largest compromises of citizen data in European history if verified. The exposure of medical conditions and banking details could facilitate targeted fraud, identity theft, and social engineering attacks against millions of individuals.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by security researchers or official statements from CNAM or French authorities.

🚨Alleged Daryn.online Breach Exposes 4.2M Kazakh User Records  A darkweb forum post claims the exposure of 4.2 million user records from daryn.online, a prominent educational platform in Kazakhstan. The listing, posted on an underground forum, alleges that a comprehensive database containing personal information, credentials, and academic data for students, teachers, and administrators has been compromised.  According to the post, the leaked dataset includes sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) and authentication details. The actor lists the following fields as exposed:  *   Full names (first and last) *   Phone numbers and email addresses *   Password hashes (bcrypt format) *   Individual Identification Numbers (IIN) *   Birthdates and gender *   User roles (student, teacher, admin) *   Academic metrics (grades, ratings, lesson counts) *   Last known IP addresses  The source material includes sample entries showing hashed passwords, active session tokens, and detailed profiles of educators, including one administrator with the email "admin6@mail.ru" and another with "support@daryn.online". The data also reveals internal identifiers such as user_id, school_id, and region_id, suggesting a structured database dump rather than a scraped surface-level leak.  Daryn.online is a significant player in Kazakhstan's EdTech sector, providing online tutoring and preparation for national university entrance exams. The exposure of millions of records, including minors' data and teacher credentials, poses significant privacy risks and potential for credential stuffing or phishing attacks against the educational community.  At the time of reporting, the alleged breach has not been independently verified by Daryn.online or security researchers.

sample_tweets.text: 🚨 Doxbin Administrator “KT” Sentenced to Prison  Callum Dare, a Doxbin administrator known online as “KT” and “Chans,” has been sentenced in Wales to two years and three months in prison for encouraging swatting attacks.  * Dare encouraged and assisted swatting incidents targeting victims in the UK, U.S., and Canada. * He created and shared compilation videos of armed police responses to promote further attacks. * Investigators linked Doxbin activity to a PayPal account and email address that identified him. * Police also recovered phishing software designed to imitate dark-web marketplaces and steal credentials.  Analyst Note: Swatting is not an online prank. False reports can trigger armed police deployments, place innocent people in immediate danger, and divert emergency resources from genuine incidents. This case also shows how small operational-security mistakes can expose administrators behind anonymous cybercrime platforms.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Doxbin

🚨 EU and UK Launch First Joint Cyber Sanctions Against Russia  The European Union and the United Kingdom have announced their first coordinated cyber sanctions package, targeting Russian intelligence officers, hackers, and organizations accused of years of cyberattacks and hybrid operations across Europe.  * 🇪🇺 EU sanctioned 9 individuals and 4 entities. * 🇬🇧 UK sanctioned 24 individuals and organizations. * Targets include GRU officers, cyber operators, and infrastructure supporting Russian cyber operations. * Measures include asset freezes, travel bans, and financial restrictions.  Analyst Note: This marks one of the strongest coordinated Western responses to state-sponsored cyber operations. Rather than focusing solely on malware campaigns, the sanctions target the broader ecosystem; including intelligence officers, cybercriminal proxies, and enabling organizations, signaling a continued shift toward imposing tangible costs on offensive cyber activity.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Russia

🇫🇷 TotalEnergies Customer Database Allegedly Offered for Sale  A threat actor has advertised the alleged sale of a TotalEnergies customer database on an underground forum, claiming the dataset contains approximately 20 million customer records.  According to the forum post:  * The database allegedly contains around 20 million customer records. * Claimed fields include names, phone numbers, addresses, postal codes, municipalities, customer categories, supplier identifiers, tariff plans, contracted power, and other account-related information. * The seller shared sample records purportedly linked to TotalEnergies France. * The dataset is being advertised for sale for €650.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, the exposure could affect a significant number of TotalEnergies customers and increase the risk of phishing, identity fraud, and targeted social engineering. Organizations should validate the authenticity of the samples, investigate the source of the data, and notify affected parties if necessary.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇦🇷 Argentine Judiciary Data Allegedly Leaked by Threat Actor  A threat actor claiming affiliation with Jama'at Al has posted what they describe as a leak of sensitive records from Argentina's judiciary system.  According to the post, the exposed data allegedly includes:  * Full names * Case and file numbers * Incident numbers * Document identifiers * Associated records * Account IDs and account creation dates * Jurisdiction information  The threat actor claims the data originates from restricted judicial records and attributes the intrusion to one of its sub-groups.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If authentic, the exposure of judicial records could present significant privacy, legal, and operational risks for affected individuals and government entities. Authorities should investigate the claims, assess the authenticity of the shared data, and determine whether any unauthorized access occurred.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Argentina

Ransomware groups increasingly rebrand to evade tracking and enforcement  A new analysis describes accelerating rebranding among ransomware and extortion groups, with operators changing names, infrastructure, payment methods, and business models while retaining operational continuity. The report cites overlaps among several ostensibly separate brands and notes 1,885 ransomware and extortion attacks during the previous three months.  #DDW #Intelligence #ransomware #wsj

🇫🇷 Tech-in-Order Database Allegedly Leaked on Underground Forum  A threat actor has published what they claim is the database of Tech-in-Order's management platform (mcs.tech-in-order.com), making the dataset available for download on an underground forum.  According to the post:  * The leaked database allegedly contains 1,376 user accounts. * A sample SQL dump was shared as proof of the claimed compromise. * The exposed data appears to include user account information and authentication-related records. * The full database is being distributed to forum members.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. Organizations using the affected platform should investigate the authenticity of the leaked data, review authentication logs for suspicious activity, rotate credentials if necessary, and monitor for any signs of unauthorized access.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #CyberSecurity

🇻🇪 Venezuelan Maritime Authority Allegedly Breached, 127K+ Records Claimed  A threat actor has published a post claiming to have compromised Venezuela's National Institute of Aquatic Spaces (INEA), alleging the theft of sensitive government and maritime sector data.  According to the post:  * 114,265 mariner records were allegedly obtained, including personal information and photographs. * 13,485 maritime company records were also reportedly compromised. * The actor claims the dataset includes legal documents, company information, and employee records. * Proof-of-concept (PoC) samples were shared on the underground forum to support the claim.  Analyst Note: These claims have not been independently verified. If confirmed, the exposure could impact both individual mariners and organizations operating within Venezuela's maritime sector. Government agencies and affected organizations should investigate the claims, validate the authenticity of the shared samples, and monitor for potential misuse of the exposed data.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Venezuela

🇫🇷 France Pare-Brise Claims New Leak Surface on Underground Forum  A threat actor has published a new post claiming to have compromised France Pare-Brise through an alleged IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference) vulnerability.  According to the forum post:  * The actor claims to have scraped data from more than 100,000 users. * Around 5,000 complete user files were allegedly leaked as proof of compromise. * The post attributes the exposure to an IDOR vulnerability affecting customer records. * The actor claims the leaked files contain complete user documents related to the alleged breach.  Analyst Note: These claims remain unverified at this time. However, if confirmed, an IDOR vulnerability could allow unauthorized access to sensitive customer information without requiring traditional authentication bypasses. Organizations should investigate any reports promptly, review access controls, and monitor for signs of exploitation.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #France

🇺🇸 MC Hypermine Database Resurfaces on Underground Forum  A threat actor has reposted the MC Hypermine database, a breach originally linked to the U.S.-based Minecraft server MC Hypermine.  According to the forum post:  * The dataset allegedly contains approximately 269,500 user records. * Exposed data reportedly includes usernames, SHA-1 password hashes, and IP addresses. * The breach is stated to have originally occurred in June 2020. * The actor is redistributing the dataset on an underground forum, making historical compromised data accessible once again.  Analyst Note: While this appears to be a re-release of an older breach rather than a new incident, recycled databases remain valuable to threat actors for credential stuffing, password-cracking campaigns, and victim profiling. Users who may have reused passwords from older accounts should ensure those credentials have been changed and that MFA is enabled wherever possible.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #USA

🇧🇪 Betrail.run Allegedly Suffers Data Exposure Affecting 2.8 Million Runner Records  A threat actor has published what they claim is a partial database from Betrail.run, a Belgium-based platform used by trail running enthusiasts to discover races, track results, and monitor performance.  According to the forum post:  * The leaked dataset allegedly contains approximately 2.8 million runner records. * The actor claims the "runners.jsonl" file contains publicly available runner information, while additional files were obtained by exploiting vulnerabilities. * The dataset is said to primarily include records from France, Belgium, Spain, the United States, Italy, Switzerland, Germany, the Netherlands, Australia, Canada, and the United Kingdom. * No technical evidence has yet been released to independently verify the alleged vulnerability or the authenticity of the non-public data.  Analyst Note: At this stage, these claims remain unverified. Organizations should wait for official confirmation while monitoring for any security advisories or disclosure from Betrail.run. If confirmed, users should be alert for phishing attempts and consider updating credentials if reused across multiple services.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #Belgium

🪖 Alleged "NATO TOP SECRET" Documents Offered for Sale on Underground Forum  A threat actor is advertising what they claim are classified NATO strategic documents dated 2025–2026.  According to the marketplace listing, the package allegedly contains documents related to:  * The 2026 NATO Summit in Ankara * Eastern Flank strategy and force posture * Intelligence collection and covert rotation concepts * OPLAN 41026 "Enhanced Deterrence East" * Supply chain and fuel logistics vulnerabilities * Nuclear weapon transportation doctrine * SHAPE C4ISR architecture * Allied Joint Force Command planning * Multinational submarine and anti-submarine exercise manuals  The seller labels the material as "NATO TOP SECRET" and is offering the collection for sale for approximately $2,500.  Analyst Note: There is currently no evidence that these documents are authentic or classified. Underground forums frequently advertise fabricated, outdated, publicly available, or recycled military documents to attract buyers. Any claims regarding classified NATO material should be treated with caution until independently verified.  #DDW #Intelligence #DarkWeb #NATO

llm_summary:
🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-07-15 07:55 KST
🔴 위협 점수: 9/10 (심각)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 사이버 보안 환경은 AI 기술을 악용한 공격의 고도화와 함께, Microsoft Patch Tuesday를 통해 기록적인 규모(622개)의 취약점이 공개되면서 패치 관리의 중요성이 극대화되었습니다 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/microsoft-patches-record-622-flaws.html)). 특히 공격자들은 정상적인 기업용 SaaS 연동(Salesforce 등)이나 OAuth 토큰을 악용하여 로그인 기록을 우회하는 'Living-off-the-land' 방식의 공격을 선호하고 있습니다 ([데일리시큐](https://www.dailysecu.com/news/articleView.html?idxno=207601)). 또한, 생성형 AI를 활용한 피싱 메일 제작 및 악성코드 로더 생성 사례가 급증하며 방어 체계의 근본적인 변화를 요구하고 있습니다 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94440/)).

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
*   **Cavern Manticore**: 이란 연계 APT 그룹으로, 모듈형 C2 프레임워크를 사용하여 이스라엘을 주요 타겟으로 활동 중 ([Check Point Research](https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/cavern-manticore-exposing-iran-linked-modular-c2-framework/)).
*   **Armored Likho**: 러시아 지역 조직을 타겟으로 AI 생성 로더와 Python 기반의 'BusySnake Stealer'를 사용하는 APT 캠페인 수행 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/tr/armored-likho-apt-with-busysnake-stealer/120292/)).
*   **The Gentlemen**: 제휴 모델(Affiliate model)을 통해 급성장 중인 랜섬웨어 그룹으로, 기업 내부망 침투 및 데이터 탈취 주력 ([Unit42](https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/the-gentlemen-ransomware/)).

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
*   **피싱 메일 유포**: 프로젝트 제안서 및 송금 확인서를 위장한 피싱 메일이 국내 기업을 대상으로 유포 중 ([AhnLab ASEC](https://asec.ahnlab.com/ko/94430/)).
*   **세무 위반 사칭**: 국세 당국을 사칭하여 원격제어 악성코드를 유포하는 피싱 메일 확인 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5771)).
*   **공급망 공격**: 과거 북한 연계 공격자가 npm 패키지('chai-as-init')를 위장하여 배포한 사례가 지속적으로 모니터링됨 ([이스트시큐리티 알약](https://blog.alyac.co.kr/5766)).

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
*   **CVE-2026-44747**: SAP NetWeaver ABAP, CVSS 9.9, 데이터 노출 및 변조 가능 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sap-patches-cvss-99-netweaver-abap-flaw.html)).
*   **CVE-2026-15409 / CVE-2026-15410**: SonicWall SMA1000, Zero-day 악용 중, SSRF 및 Command Injection ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/sonicwall-warns-of-sma1000-flaws-exploited-in-zero-day-attacks-patch-now/)).
*   **CVE-2026-56259**: Crawl4AI Docker API, LLM 키 탈취 가능 ([Twitter](https://twitter.com/disismohi/status/2077156943047598530)).

### 5. IOC 요약
*   **CVE**: 
    *   CVE-2026-44747 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/07/sap-patches-cvss-99-netweaver-abap-flaw.html))
    *   CVE-2026-15409, CVE-2026-15410 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/sonicwall-warns-of-sma1000-flaws-exploited-in-zero-day-attacks-patch-now/))
*   **도메인/해시**: 이번 수집 데이터 내 특정되지 않음.

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
*   **긴급 패치 적용**: SonicWall SMA1000 및 Microsoft Patch Tuesday(특히 Active Directory/SharePoint 관련) 보안 업데이트를 즉시 적용하십시오.
*   **SaaS 연동 점검**: Salesforce 등 기업용 클라우드 서비스에 연결된 외부 앱 및 권한(OAuth 토큰)을 전수 조사하여 불필요한 연결을 제거하십시오.
*   **피싱 대응 강화**: 프로젝트 제안서나 송금 확인서를 사칭한 메일 유입 시 첨부파일 실행을 엄격히 금지하고, 메일 헤더 및 발신자 도메인을 검증하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
*   **점수**: 9/10
*   **근거**: Microsoft의 기록적인 취약점 공개와 더불어 SonicWall 등 주요 네트워크 장비의 Zero-day 악용이 동시다발적으로 발생하여 기업 인프라의 즉각적인 위협이 매우 높음.

📊 Gemini 토큰: 이번 14,785개 (입력 13,443 / 출력 1,342)
📈 세션 누적: 14,785 / 1,000,000 (1.5%) | 세션 잔여 추정 985,215개

news_articles.title: 해킹조직 샤이니헌터스, 세일즈포스 ‘신뢰 연결’ 악용해 기업 정보 탈취

news_articles.title: 행안부, 고위험 취약점 긴급 패치 장애 발생해도 공무원 책임 면제
news_articles.summary: 행정안전부가 인공지능(AI) 등이 발견한 고위험 보안 취약점에 대응하기 위해 긴급 패치를 적용하다 시스템 장애가 발생하더라도, 필수 안전 조치를 준수했다면 담당 공무원의 책임을 면제하기로 했다.보안 패치 이후 발생할 수 있는 장애와 책임 문제로 공공 정보시스템 운영자들이 긴급 조치를 주저하던 문제를 줄이고, 고위험 취약점에 대한 대응 속도를 높이기 위한 조치다.행정안전부는 최근 적극행정위원회 의견 제시를 통해 정보시스템에서 고위험 취약점이 발견돼 긴급 보안 패치를 진행할 때 일정한 기준과 절차를 준수한 경우 이를 적극행정으로 인정하
